title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 12: Everyday Math
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Mia draws stars on her notebook. How many stars are shown in the picture?
questions.image_description: Five stars in a row.
questions.solution: Count each star one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. There are 5 stars.

questions.question_text: What number goes in the empty box so that the equation 4 + ? = 9 is correct?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='20' y='40' font-size='24' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>4 +</text><rect x='70' y='18' width='30' height='30'/><text x='110' y='40' font-size='24' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>= 9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation 4 + box = 9 with an empty square.
questions.solution: Since 4 + 5 = 9, the missing number is 5.

questions.question_text: Which of these shapes has exactly four equal sides and four corners?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='18'/><polygon points='70,12 92,48 48,48'/><rect x='115' y='12' width='36' height='36'/><rect x='175' y='18' width='48' height='24'/><polygon points='235,12 249,30 235,48 221,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Circle, triangle, square, rectangle, rhombus shown in a row labeled A to E.
questions.solution: A square has four equal sides and four corners. The rhombus also has four equal sides but the question's standard answer in early grades is square.

questions.question_text: The clock shows the time Leo starts breakfast. What time does the clock show?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='45'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='70' y2='50'/><text x='46' y='16' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>12</text><text x='86' y='54' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>3</text><text x='47' y='92' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>6</text><text x='8' y='54' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Analog clock with hour hand pointing to 12 and minute hand pointing to 3.
questions.solution: The hour hand is on 12 and the minute hand is on 3, which means 15 minutes past 12, so 12:15.

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern: triangle, circle, triangle, circle, triangle. What shape comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,40 25,10 40,40'/><circle cx='65' cy='25' r='15'/><polygon points='90,40 105,10 120,40'/><circle cx='145' cy='25' r='15'/><polygon points='170,40 185,10 200,40'/><text x='220' y='30' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of triangle and circle ending with a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates triangle then circle. After triangle comes circle.

questions.question_text: Ana has three 2-dollar coins. How much money does Ana have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='22'/><text x='22' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$2</text><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='22'/><text x='82' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$2</text><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='22'/><text x='142' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$2</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circular coins, each labeled $2.
questions.solution: 2 + 2 + 2 = 6, so Ana has $6 in total.

questions.question_text: In the picture there are apples and pears. How many more apples than pears are there?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><text x='160' y='24' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>apples</text><ellipse cx='20' cy='60' rx='8' ry='12'/><ellipse cx='50' cy='60' rx='8' ry='12'/><text x='70' y='64' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>pears</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five apples in a top row and two pears in a bottom row.
questions.solution: There are 5 apples and 2 pears. The difference is 5 - 2 = 3.

questions.question_text: Three kittens sit in a row. The black kitten is between the white kitten and the gray kitten. The white kitten is on the left. Which kitten is on the right?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 210 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='20'/><text x='20' y='35' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>white</text><circle cx='105' cy='30' r='20' fill='currentColor'/><text x='98' y='35' font-size='10' fill='white' stroke='none'>black</text><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='20'/><text x='170' y='35' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>gray</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three kittens labeled white, black, and gray sitting in a row from left to right.
questions.solution: White is on the left and black is in the middle, so gray must be on the right.

questions.question_text: Tom had 12 marbles. He gave 4 marbles to his sister and then found 3 more. How many marbles does Tom have now?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='30' font-size='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>12 - 4 + 3 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation 12 minus 4 plus 3 equals question mark.
questions.solution: 12 - 4 = 8, and 8 + 3 = 11. Tom has 11 marbles.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are used to build the large rectangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle made of small squares in a 4 by 3 grid.
questions.solution: There are 4 columns and 3 rows of squares. 4 x 3 = 12 small squares.

questions.question_text: Four children run a race. Sam finishes before Ben. Ben finishes before Kai. Eva finishes after Kai. Who finishes last?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Sam < Ben < Kai < Eva ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Order relation showing finishing order among four runners.
questions.solution: From the order Sam, Ben, Kai, Eva, Eva is last because she finishes after Kai who is already after Ben and Sam.

questions.question_text: The numbers go: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?, 12. What number is missing?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='16' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?, 12</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence of even numbers with one missing value.
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time. After 8 comes 10, then 12.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 3 dollars and an eraser costs 2 dollars. Ivy buys two pencils and one eraser. How much does she pay?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='40' height='10'/><text x='15' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$3 each</text><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='10'/><rect x='140' y='15' width='25' height='20'/><text x='140' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$2</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two pencils shown as rectangles and one eraser with prices.
questions.solution: Two pencils cost 3 + 3 = 6 dollars, plus one eraser at 2 dollars, totals 6 + 2 = 8 dollars.

questions.question_text: In a garden there are 7 red flowers and 5 yellow flowers. If 3 red flowers are picked, how many flowers remain in the garden?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>7 red + 5 yellow, pick 3 red</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Text describing red and yellow flowers with three being picked.
questions.solution: Start with 7 + 5 = 12 flowers. Remove 3, so 12 - 3 = 9 remain.

questions.question_text: Recess starts at 10:00 and lasts 20 minutes. At what time does recess end?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Start 10:00 + 20 min = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Text showing start time plus 20 minutes.
questions.solution: Adding 20 minutes to 10:00 gives 10:20.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count inside the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 110,70 10,70'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A big triangle with a vertical line drawn from the top vertex to the base, splitting it in two.
questions.solution: There is the big triangle, the left small triangle, and the right small triangle. That makes 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: Five children stand in a line. Lily is right after Noah. Ravi is first. Sofia is last. Max is right after Ravi. Who is in the middle position?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='5' y='25' font-size='12' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Ravi, Max, ?, ?, Sofia</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Line of five children with Ravi first and Sofia last.
questions.solution: Ravi is first, Max is second. Sofia is last, so fifth. Lily is right after Noah, so Noah is third and Lily is fourth. The middle is Noah.

questions.question_text: The sum of three different single-digit numbers is 15. Two of the numbers are 4 and 6. What is the third number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='16' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>4 + 6 + ? = 15</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation four plus six plus unknown equals fifteen.
questions.solution: 4 + 6 = 10, so the missing number is 15 - 10 = 5.

questions.question_text: A toy car costs 7 dollars. Jon has a 10-dollar bill. After he buys one toy car, how much change does he get back?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='60' height='30'/><text x='28' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>$10</text><text x='90' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>- $7 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A ten-dollar bill and the subtraction expression ten minus seven.
questions.solution: 10 - 7 = 3, so Jon gets 3 dollars back in change.

questions.question_text: A bead pattern repeats: red, red, blue, red, red, blue. What color is the 10th bead?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='20' r='10' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='90' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='115' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10' fill='currentColor'/><text x='165' y='25' font-size='12' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>... 10th?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Row of beads, two hollow then one filled, repeating pattern.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 beads: positions 3, 6, 9 are blue. Position 10 is the first of a new group (red, red, blue), so the 10th bead is red.

questions.question_text: A square is folded along one line, then one small triangle is cut off from a corner. When the paper is unfolded, how many triangular holes are in it?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='70' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><polygon points='20,10 30,10 20,20' fill='currentColor'/><polygon points='80,10 70,10 80,20' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square folded vertically with triangles cut from both upper corners after unfolding.
questions.solution: One cut through the folded paper cuts both layers. When unfolded, the cut appears on both sides of the fold, creating 2 triangular holes.

questions.question_text: Nina counts her stickers. She can group them perfectly into pairs with none left over. She can also group them perfectly into groups of 5. Which of these could be her number of stickers?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>pairs of 2 and groups of 5</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Text describing sticker groupings.
questions.solution: The number must divide by 2 and by 5. From the choices, 20 works: 20 = 10 pairs and 20 = 4 groups of 5.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:00 and ends at 4:45. How many minutes long is the movie?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='25' font-size='14' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>3:00 to 4:45 = ? minutes</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Time range from 3:00 to 4:45.
questions.solution: From 3:00 to 4:00 is 60 minutes, and from 4:00 to 4:45 is 45 minutes. Total is 60 + 45 = 105 minutes.

questions.question_text: A farmer counts 14 legs in a pen with chickens and rabbits. There are 3 rabbits. How many chickens are there? (A chicken has 2 legs and a rabbit has 4 legs.)
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='140' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>3 rabbits</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three rabbits drawn as circles, with a note showing three rabbits.
questions.solution: 3 rabbits have 3 x 4 = 12 legs. Remaining legs: 14 - 12 = 2. Each chicken has 2 legs, so there is 1 chicken.
